name: Falscher Hase (False Hare (German Meatloaf))
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix ground beef and pork, onion, bread crumbs, 3 tablespoons cold water, and eggs in a large bowl.
2. Add salt, paprika, mustard, and parsley; blend until all ingredients are thoroughly combined.
3. Form the mixture into a square shape (8 X 8 inches) and place whole hard-boiled eggs in a row along the middle.
4. Fold the sides of the meat over the eggs to create a loaf that resembles flat bread.
5. Lightly rinse hands with cold water to prevent sticking.
6. Cube two strips of bacon; cook in a Dutch oven for about 2 minutes.
7. Add vegetable oil and heat the pot. Place the meatloaf in the Dutch oven and cook until browned on all sides.
8. Cut remaining bacon into halves and arrange over the top of the meatloaf.
9. Transfer the Dutch oven to a preheated oven and bake for about 45 minutes.
10. While baking, gradually pour hot beef broth over the meatloaf and brush with pan drippings occasionally.
11. Once done, remove the meatloaf to a warmed platter and keep it warm.
12. In the same pot, add 1/4 cup hot water and scrape any particles from the bottom. Bring to a gentle boil and stir in cornstarch mixed with 1/4 cup cold water. Cook until bubbly and thick.
13. Remove from heat and stir in sour cream.
14. Season with salt and pepper if desired. Serve the sauce separately.
